message:
fr-store: make TOUCH and SORT update LRU and keyspace stats

Bring Store::touch and Store::sort_elements in line with Redis
semantics: both commands must count as keyspace lookups and, on hit,
update last_access_ms so they drive LRU eviction and INFO
keyspace_hits / keyspace_misses counters.

Store::touch:
- Replace the bare drop_if_expired + contains_key dance with a call
  to record_keyspace_lookup(key, now_ms). That helper drops the entry
  if it is expired and increments either stat_keyspace_hits or
  stat_keyspace_misses.
- On hit, look up the entry with entries.get_mut and call
  entry.touch(now_ms) so the access timestamp advances.
- The returned count continues to reflect only the keys that actually
  exist after expiry handling.
- TOUCH on a missing key now increments stat_keyspace_misses instead
  of being silently invisible to INFO stats.

Store::sort_elements:
- Front-load the same record_keyspace_lookup call. If it returns
  false (expired or missing), return an empty Vec — the pre-existing
  "returns empty vec if key does not exist" contract.
- For the two container arms that did not already call entry.touch
  (Value::List and Value::Set), call entry.touch(now_ms) after
  collecting the elements so SORT registers as an access. The
  SortedSet arm was already touching and is unchanged.
- The WrongType path for string/hash/stream is unaffected.

Test: touch_and_sort_update_lru_and_keyspace_stats
- Seed a list "list" and a set "set" at now_ms=10, reset INFO stats.
- Assert both entries have last_access_ms == 10 after the mutations.
- touch(&["list","missing"], 100) -> returns 1, advances list's
  last_access_ms to 100, hits=1, misses=1.
- sort_elements(&"set", 200) -> returns ["member"], advances set's
  last_access_ms to 200, hits=2, misses=1.

Leaving untracked: .ntm/, the pile of test_*.sh/.rs/bin, fix_*.py,
refactor_*.py, extract*.py, count_panics.py, panics.txt, ubs.sarif,
ubs_out.txt, dump.rdb, scripts/__pycache__/, lua_test*, and other
scratch artifacts from the bug-hunting session.
